overview.headline: Connect the data estate, catalog and enrich it, map lineage end to end, and hand over the findings.
overview.background: BPC connects the source systems, cloud platform, and models the assessment runs on, ingests metadata, enriches it with usage context, maps end-to-end lineage, and packages findings and recommendations for handover.
overview.outcome: Findings, lineage, and recommendations are delivered as final outputs the client owns.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.summary: Every connection the assessment runs on, categorized into Sources, Cloud, and LLM, with live connectivity and usage.

subparts.name: Sources
subparts.desc: Source systems the assessment reads from.

subparts.name: LLM
subparts.desc: Models driving ingestion, scoring, and classification.

layers.name: Collection
layers.summary: Discovery and metadata ingestion: domain intelligence, an automated catalog, enrichment, and transformation analysis.

subparts.name: Domain Intelligence
subparts.desc: Business domain context and data ownership captured with subject-matter experts.

demoIntel.title: BPC inference
demoIntel.note: Inferred by BPC from schema, table names, and value distributions - no SME input.

items.text: This is an enterprise data-warehousing operation: the estate is fact/dimension modeled with staging, conformed, and mart schemas - a classic Bronze/Silver/Gold warehouse, not a transactional system.
Value concentrates in a handful of conformed fact tables - a small set of wide fact tables carries most rows and joins to shared conformed dimensions (date, customer, product, geography).
Heavy ELT footprint - hundreds of pipelines and staging tables feed the marts, so transformation logic and load order are the main lineage complexity.
Conformed dimensions and customer marts carry PII (name, contact, address) - governs Silver-layer masking and access policy.

subparts.name: Automated Data Catalog
subparts.desc: Automated ingestion of tables, schemas, columns, and sizes.

subparts.name: Data Enrichment
subparts.desc: Usage patterns, access tools, and frequency from log analysis.

demoIntel.title: BPC data annotation
demoIntel.note: Business meaning BPC attached to each asset, with the evidence it used to decide.

items.note: Central conformed sales fact - the warehouse's revenue grain.
items.why: Queried by 40+ BI reports/day, joins to every conformed dimension, freshness under 1h.

items.note: Conformed customer master shared across marts.
items.why: Contains name, email, address; referenced by 20+ downstream marts - governance flag set.

items.note: Raw landing / staging copies feeding the ELT.
items.why: Transient, reprocessed each load, no direct BI consumption - stays Bronze, not promoted.

items.note: Staging tables with no downstream lineage.
items.why: Zero reads in 90 days and no consuming pipeline - deprecation candidates for the backlog.

subparts.name: Transformation
subparts.desc: Pipeline parsing and ETL / database assessment of transformation logic.

demoMeaning.title: What does the data mean?
demoMeaning.subtitle: BPC reads the catalog and answers in business terms - not just what the tables are, but what they say about the business.
demoMeaning.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

questions.q: What business is this data describing?
questions.a: A large enterprise data-warehouse operation. The catalog is fact/dimension modeled across staging, conformed, and mart layers - the analytical backbone that BI and reporting run on, spanning 11 business domains.

In the conformed fact tables and shared dimensions. A small set of wide sales, finance, and operations facts carry most rows and every important join, so those and the conformed dimensions are what to promote to Gold first.

questions.q: What does the data warn us about?
questions.a: Sprawl and PII. Hundreds of staging and orphaned tables inflate the estate without downstream value, and conformed customer dimensions carry PII that must be masked before the Silver layer.

layers.name: Unification
layers.summary: End-to-end lineage across datasets and reference systems, with ownership mapping and manual additions where automation is limited.

subparts.name: End-to-End Visualization
subparts.desc: Lineage from upstream sources through transformations to downstream consumption.

subparts.name: Manual Additions
subparts.desc: Analyst-added lineage where API or metadata access is limited.

demoSignal.title: Signal that matters most
demoSignal.subtitle: Across the lineage, ownership, and manual-addition work, BPC ranks which signal to act on first for the classification decision.
demoSignal.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

primary.why: The number of certified BI consumers hanging off a dataset predicts Gold candidacy better than row count or size. The conformed sales and finance facts fan out the widest, so their lineage pays back first.

ranked.why: An unowned dataset can't be promoted or governed; unresolved ownership is the gating risk for classification sign-off.
Staging-to-mart load order defines what breaks when a source shifts - the main place automated lineage needs analyst confirmation.
Shared dimensions feed every mart - stale conformed master data ripples across the whole warehouse but changes slowly.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.summary: The face of the assessment: classification, alignment, simulation, backlog, and enablement, packaged for the client to act and own.

subparts.name: Classification & Alignment
subparts.desc: Dataset classification and current-state alignment baseline.

subparts.name: Impact & Backlog
subparts.desc: Cost/performance simulation and prioritized transition backlog.

deliverables.purpose: Validation of source access and any connectivity limitations

deliverables.name: Metadata Inventory
deliverables.purpose: Centralized catalog of sources and metadata

demoProcessing.title: How BPC processed this estate
demoProcessing.subtitle: The Business Process Co-Pilot runs every asset through the same pipeline. This is what it did with the data-warehouse catalog.
demoProcessing.author: BPC · Business Process Co-Pilot

stages.name: Ingest
stages.detail: Read schemas, columns, and row counts from every connected source.

stages.name: Infer
stages.detail: Derived the warehouse model and domain map from names and value distributions.

stages.name: Annotate
stages.detail: Tagged PII, conformed facts, staging, and deprecation candidates with reasoning.

stages.name: Classify
stages.detail: Scored each dataset to Bronze / Silver / Gold against usage and lineage.

stages.name: Recommend
stages.detail: Prioritized transition backlog and executive recommendations.

confidence.label: Model confidence on this estate
confidence.detail: Weighted across inference, annotation, and classification passes.
